topic: Paper 1: Exploring Modern and Literary Heritage Texts
subject: english-literature

items.q: What is the difference between a theme and a symbol?
items.correct: A theme is an abstract idea or concern the text explores (e.g. mortality); a symbol is a concrete image that stands for something else (e.g. a skull).
items.misconception: Themes are just symbols repeated through the book.
items.random_wrong: A theme is the title of the book.
items.why: Students treat theme and symbol as the same thing. Themes are ideas, symbols are images. Skulls (symbol) can develop the theme (mortality), but they are not the same level of abstraction.

items.correct: When a speaker says one thing but means another — typically the opposite — relying on shared context for the listener to detect the gap.
items.misconception: Verbal irony is just saying something unexpected.
items.random_wrong: Verbal irony is when you make a joke about something.
items.why: Students conflate irony with surprise or humour. Irony specifically involves a gap between literal and intended meaning — a more precise device than 'unexpected'.

items.q: What are conventions of the Gothic genre?
items.correct: Settings of ruin and decay, supernatural or psychological terror, transgression of boundaries, the sublime, doubled or haunted protagonists, and atmospheric excess.
items.misconception: Gothic just means scary stories with vampires and ghosts.
items.random_wrong: Gothic refers to medieval architecture, not literature.
items.why: Students reduce Gothic to monsters. The genre is defined by its concerns — boundaries, the past intruding on the present, the limits of reason — and only sometimes uses supernatural creatures.
It's Brecht. “Beckett” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Who wrote A Streetcar Named Desire?
items.why: It's Williams. “Miller” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: In what year was Waiting for Godot first performed?
items.why: The real figure is 1953 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
